title: Woodland Wins Houston Open
excerpt: Gary Woodland claims Texas Children's Houston Open title, Nicolai Højgaard finishes second
body:
Texas Children's Houston Open Recap

Gary Woodland has taken home the top prize at the Texas Children's Houston Open, finishing with a score of -18. The American golfer played consistently well throughout the tournament, with rounds of 64, 63, and 65.

Leaderboard Highlights

Nicolai Højgaard came in second place with a score of -17, while Min Woo Lee and Michael Thorbjornsen tied for third at -12. The leaderboard saw several notable players, including Jason Day and Adam Scott, who finished tied for fifth and tenth respectively.

DP World Tour and LIV Golf Updates

In other golf news, the DP World Tour has announced its schedule for the upcoming months, with the Hero Indian Open and THE MASTERS taking place in April. Meanwhile, the LIV Golf tour recently held its Singapore 2026 event, which saw 4Aces GC take the top spot with a score of -27.

Statistics from the Tournament

Lowest Round
63
Most Birdies
24
Longest Drive
417 yards

What's Next for Golf Fans

With several high-profile tournaments on the horizon, golf fans have a lot to look forward to in the coming weeks. The DP World Tour's Asian swing is set to continue, while the PGA Tour will be heading to some of its most iconic courses. Whether you're following the action on the course or keeping an eye on the latest golf news, there's never been a more exciting time to be a golf fan.

The Texas Children's Houston Open has once again proven itself to be one of the most exciting events on the golf calendar, with a talented field and a challenging course.
